Include the stakeholder's connection to the community, their specific endorsement, and how they will support or benefit from the project. Label clearly as DRAFT — TO BE CUSTOMIZED AND SIGNED.
required_documents.upload_instructions: After generating a draft, print it, have the partner review and edit it, then get it signed on their letterhead. Upload the signed version here.

davis_bacon.description: Not typically applicable unless federal construction funds are involved.

section_3.description: Not applicable unless HUD funds are involved.

nepa.description: Not applicable for most community foundation grants.

buy_america.description: Not applicable.

single_audit.description: Not applicable for most community/local grants unless federal funds are involved.

irb.description: Not applicable.

matching.description: Many community grants value matching funds or in-kind support but may not require it.

indirect_cost_rules.notes: Community grants from local foundations typically do not use indirect cost rate structures. Keep overhead minimal and direct most funds to project activities.

submission_portal.name: Various
submission_portal.notes: Community foundations, local government grant programs, and neighborhood development organizations each have their own application processes.

community_project.ai_context: Community project grants emphasize demonstrated community need (with data), community engagement and support, and sustainable impact. Letters of support from community stakeholders are critical. Show how the project addresses an urgent local need and has buy-in from residents. Sustainability plan should explain how benefits continue after the grant period. Budget should maximize direct community benefit.

formatting_rules.notes: Default formatting. 12pt Times New Roman, 1-inch margins, single-spaced.

education.name: Department of Education (ED)

prerequisites.name: SAM.gov Registration
prerequisites.description: Active registration at SAM.gov is required. Allow 4+ weeks for new registrations.

prerequisites.name: Unique Entity Identifier (UEI)
prerequisites.description: 12-character identifier from SAM.gov, replaced DUNS in April 2022.

prerequisites.name: Grants.gov Account
prerequisites.description: Registration on Grants.gov with Authorized Organization Representative (AOR).

prerequisites.name: G5 System Registration
prerequisites.description: ED electronic grants management system for post-award management.

required_sections.name: Project Abstract
required_sections.guidance: One-page summary: project title, absolute priority, target population, summary of activities, expected outcomes, name of subgrantees (if known).

required_sections.name: Project Narrative
required_sections.guidance: Must address absolute, competitive and invitational priorities. Include: (1) need/population, (2) objectives, (3) methods, (4) management plan, (5) evaluation, (6) supporting documents.

required_sections.name: Budget
required_sections.guidance: Complete ED Form 524. Include all costs by category. Most ED programs require cost sharing/matching - document clearly.

required_sections.name: Budget Justification
required_sections.guidance: Justify all costs: personnel (salary, fringe), travel, equipment, supplies, subcontracts, indirect costs. Show calculation methods.

required_sections.name: Management Plan
required_sections.guidance: Show project governance: key personnel, timeline, milestones, responsibilities. Demonstrate capacity to implement.

required_sections.name: Evaluation Plan
required_sections.guidance: Describe evaluation design: logic model, research questions, data collection, analysis methods, how outcomes will be measured.

required_sections.name: Response to Prior Feedback
required_sections.guidance: If you received prior ED award: explain how you addressed any weaknesses identified in prior reviews.

required_documents.name: SF-424 Application for Federal Assistance
required_documents.description: Standard federal application form required for all Department of Education grant submissions
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate SF-424 Application for Federal Assistance for a Department of Education grant

required_documents.name: SF-424A Budget Information - Non-Construction Programs
required_documents.description: Standard federal budget form detailing costs by category and funding period
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate SF-424A Budget Information form for a Department of Education grant

required_documents.name: SF-424B Assurances - Non-Construction Programs
required_documents.description: Standard assurances for non-construction programs requiring authorized representative signature
required_documents.upload_instructions: Download SF-424B from Grants.gov, have your Authorized Representative sign it, and upload the signed form.

required_documents.name: GEPA Section 427 Statement
required_documents.description: Statement describing steps the applicant will take to ensure equitable access to and participation in the project for students, teachers, and other beneficiaries with special needs -- uniquely required by the Department of Education
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate a GEPA Section 427 equitable access statement for a Department of Education grant application

required_documents.name: Program Narrative
required_documents.description: Detailed narrative addressing all absolute, competitive, and invitational priorities from the NOFO, including need, objectives, methods, management, and evaluation
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate a program narrative for a Department of Education grant addressing NOFO priorities

required_documents.name: Budget Narrative (ED Form 524 Support)
required_documents.description: Detailed narrative justifying all costs by category with calculation methods, supporting the ED Form 524 budget
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate a budget narrative for a Department of Education grant justifying all cost categories

required_documents.name: Key Personnel Qualifications
required_documents.description: Resumes, CVs, or qualifications summaries for all key project personnel demonstrating relevant education and experience
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate key personnel qualifications summaries for a Department of Education grant application

required_documents.name: Needs Assessment Data
required_documents.description: Supporting data demonstrating the educational need including demographics, achievement gaps, test scores, and other relevant statistics
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload needs assessment data supporting the statement of need. Include relevant local data, demographics, achievement metrics, and comparison data. Data must be from verifiable sources.

davis_bacon.description: ED grants rarely involve construction.

section_3.description: HUD requirement. Not applicable to ED.

nepa.description: ED grants typically do not trigger NEPA review.

buy_america.description: Not applicable to ED grants.

single_audit.description: Organizations spending $1M+ in federal funds per year must have a Single Audit.

irb.description: IRB approval required for research grants involving human subjects.

matching.description: Varies by program. Some competitive programs require cost sharing. Check specific NOFO.

ferpa.description: Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act protects student education records. Annual notification required. Written consent needed before releasing PII. Violations can result in loss of all federal funding.

edgar.description: Education Department General Administrative Regulations (34 CFR Parts 75-99) govern all ED discretionary grants. Prior approval required for budget changes >10%.

supplement_not_supplant.description: Federal funds must ADD TO state/local spending, not replace it. Applies to Title I, IDEA, Title II Part A, Title III. Restricted indirect cost rate required.

gepa_427.description: GEPA Section 427 statement required -- describe steps to ensure equitable access for special populations. Uniquely required by ED.

indirect_cost_rules.notes: ED uses 8% de minimis for organizations without a negotiated rate (lower than government-wide 15%). Restricted indirect cost rate required for supplement-not-supplant programs (Title I, IDEA). Must submit rate proposal within 90 days of Grant Award Notification.

submission_portal.name: Grants.gov / G5 Portal
submission_portal.notes: Applications submitted through Grants.gov. Post-award management through G5 system. ED has been subject to restructuring in 2025-2026 which may affect administration.

education.ai_context: ED proposals must address absolute, competitive, and invitational priorities from the NOFO. Logic models or theories of change are typically required. FERPA compliance is critical for any project involving student data. GEPA Section 427 statement (equitable access for special populations) is uniquely required by ED. Supplement-not-supplant rules apply to formula programs with restricted indirect cost rates. ED uses 8% de minimis rate (lower than the standard 15%). EDGAR governs all discretionary grants. Title I uses four different allocation formulas. Higher ed institutions need special eligibility designations (HSI, HBCU, TCU, AANAPISI) for certain programs.

formatting_rules.notes: Double-spaced per EDGAR requirements. 1-inch margins. 12pt font.

hud.name: HUD (Housing and Urban Development)
hud.full_name: Department of Housing and Urban Development

prerequisites.name: SAM.gov Registration
prerequisites.description: Active registration at SAM.gov is required for all federal grants.

prerequisites.name: Unique Entity Identifier (UEI)
prerequisites.description: 12-character identifier from SAM.gov, replaced DUNS in April 2022.

prerequisites.name: Grants.gov Account
prerequisites.description: Registration on Grants.gov with AOR.

prerequisites.name: HUD-2880 Applicant Disclosure
prerequisites.description: HUD-specific Applicant/Recipient Disclosure/Update Report.

prerequisites.name: Consolidated Plan
prerequisites.description: 3-5 year strategic plan required for CDBG/HOME entitlement communities. Includes Annual Action Plan and Citizen Participation Plan.

required_sections.name: Statement of Need
required_sections.guidance: Describe the housing/community development need. Use data: demographics, gaps, conditions. Explain why federal funds are needed.

required_sections.name: Project Description
required_sections.guidance: Describe: (1) activities/services, (2) target population, (3) expected outcomes, (4) how addresses need, (5) timeline, (6) partnerships.

required_sections.name: Budget
required_sections.guidance: Complete detailed budget. HUD programs often require 25-50% match - document match sources clearly.

required_sections.name: Budget Justification
required_sections.guidance: Explain each line item. Show how costs are reasonable and necessary. Address any match requirements.

required_sections.name: Logic Model
required_sections.guidance: Show: inputs > activities > outputs > outcomes. Connect your activities to measurable short/long-term outcomes.

required_sections.name: Organizational Capacity
required_sections.guidance: Describe: organizational experience, key staff qualifications, past performance, governance, ability to manage federal funds.

required_sections.name: Leverage/Resources
required_sections.guidance: Document other resources: matching funds, in-kind contributions, partnerships that strengthen the project.

required_documents.name: HUD-2880 Applicant/Recipient Disclosure/Update Report
required_documents.description: HUD-specific disclosure form reporting all financial assistance received and pending. Must be kept updated.
required_documents.upload_instructions: Complete and upload HUD-2880. Discloses all financial assistance received and pending from HUD and other sources.

required_documents.name: HUD-424-B Assurances and Certifications
required_documents.description: HUD-specific assurances replacing SF-424B. Covers civil rights certifications, nondiscrimination, and compliance assurances.
required_documents.upload_instructions: Complete and sign HUD-424-B Assurances and Certifications. Covers civil rights, Title VI, Title VIII, Section 504, and Age Discrimination Act.

required_documents.name: HUD-424-CB Detailed Budget Form
required_documents.description: HUD-specific detailed budget form with entity information and subrecipient certifications.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate HUD-424-CB Detailed Budget Form from budget data with entity information and cost categories

required_documents.name: HUD-424-CBW Detailed Budget Worksheet
required_documents.description: Worksheet supporting the HUD-424-CB with line-item detail for each budget category.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate HUD-424-CBW Detailed Budget Worksheet with line-item detail supporting the HUD-424-CB

required_documents.name: Affirmatively Furthering Fair Housing Certification
required_documents.description: Certification that applicant will affirmatively further fair housing per Fair Housing Act and EO 12892.
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload your signed Affirmatively Furthering Fair Housing Certification.

required_documents.name: Match / Leverage Documentation
required_documents.description: Documentation of committed matching funds. HOME requires 25% match. Most competitive HUD programs score leveraged resources heavily.
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload documentation of all committed matching funds and leveraged resources. Include commitment letters from each match source.

required_documents.name: MOU with CHDO or Service Provider
required_documents.description: Memorandum of Understanding with Community Housing Development Organization or service provider partner.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) between [applicant organization] and [CHDO or service provider partner] for a HUD housing project. Include the purpose of the partnership, roles and responsibilities of each party, duration of the agreement, scope of services, and any resource commitments. Label clearly as DRAFT — TO BE CUSTOMIZED AND SIGNED BY BOTH PARTIES.
required_documents.upload_instructions: After generating a draft, print it, have both parties review and edit it, then get it signed by authorized representatives from both organizations on official letterhead. Upload the signed version here.

required_documents.name: Environmental Review Record
required_documents.description: Environmental review documentation per 24 CFR Part 58. HUD uniquely delegates environmental review to the grantee.
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload your Environmental Review Record (ERR) per 24 CFR Part 58. No funds may be committed until review complete and HUD issues Release of Funds.

required_documents.name: Citizen Participation Plan
required_documents.description: Plan describing how citizens will be involved in the project planning and implementation.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft a Citizen Participation Plan for a HUD-funded community development project

required_documents.name: Board Resolution
required_documents.description: Board authorization to apply for this HUD grant and accept funding obligations.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft a board resolution authorizing application for a HUD grant

davis_bacon.description: Must pay workers prevailing wages on all construction/rehabilitation contracts over $2,000 funded by HUD per 40 USC 3141-3148.

section_3.description: Must direct employment, training, and contracting opportunities to low-income residents and Section 3 businesses per 24 CFR Part 75. Thresholds: $200K for housing/community development, $100K for lead hazard.

nepa.description: Environmental review required per 24 CFR Part 58. Uniquely, HUD delegates this responsibility to the grant recipient (not HUD itself). No funds may be committed until environmental review is complete and HUD issues a Release of Funds.

buy_america.description: Not a standard HUD requirement, though BABA may apply to infrastructure components.

single_audit.description: Organizations spending $1M+ in federal funds per year must have a Single Audit.

irb.description: Not applicable to HUD grants.

matching.description: CDBG entitlement: no formal match but leveraging scored. HOME: 25% match (with reductions for fiscal distress). Section 202/811: varies. Most competitive HUD programs score leveraged resources heavily.

fair_housing.description: Must affirmatively further fair housing per Fair Housing Act and EO 12892. Not just avoid discrimination but take proactive steps to promote equal housing opportunity.

ura.description: Uniform Relocation Assistance Act protects people displaced by federally-funded projects. Must provide relocation assistance and fair compensation per 49 CFR Part 24.

indirect_cost_rules.notes: HUD accepts negotiated NICRA or de minimis 15%. CDBG has administrative caps: 20% for planning/admin, with admin capped at 5% of total grant.

submission_portal.name: Grants.gov / HUD Exchange
submission_portal.notes: Applications submitted via Grants.gov. HUD Exchange (hudexchange.info) provides technical assistance and post-award resources. JustGrants is NOT used by HUD.

hud.ai_context: HUD proposals must address housing and community development needs with strong data. CDBG is an entitlement program for metropolitan cities (50,000+) and urban counties -- formula-based, not competitive. HOME funds go to Participating Jurisdictions with 15% CHDO set-aside. Davis-Bacon prevailing wage requirements apply to all construction over $2,000. Section 3 requires directing employment to low-income residents. Environmental review is uniquely delegated to the grantee (not HUD). Fair housing obligations are proactive, not just non-discrimination. Matching/leveraging is heavily scored in competitive programs.

formatting_rules.notes: Single or 1.5 spacing depending on program. Check specific NOFA for spacing requirements.

nasa.name: NASA (National Aeronautics and Space Administration)

prerequisites.name: SAM.gov Registration
prerequisites.description: Active registration at SAM.gov is required for all federal grants.

prerequisites.name: Unique Entity Identifier (UEI)
prerequisites.description: 12-character identifier from SAM.gov, replaced DUNS in April 2022.

prerequisites.name: NSPIRES Registration
prerequisites.description: NASA Solicitation and Proposal Integrated Review and Evaluation System required for research proposals.

prerequisites.name: Export Control Registration
prerequisites.description: ITAR-controlled work may require State Department DDTC registration. EAR-controlled work may need BIS classification.

required_sections.name: Technical Abstract
required_sections.guidance: Summary of project: objectives, methods, expected results, NASA relevance. Must be suitable for public release.

required_sections.name: Technical Proposal
required_sections.guidance: Include: (1) objectives/significance, (2) approach/methodology, (3) innovation, (4) work plan/timeline, (5) deliverables, (6) relevance to NASA mission.

required_sections.name: Key Personnel
required_sections.guidance: Describe qualifications: education, experience, publications, relevant NASA/applied research. Show team expertise.

required_sections.name: Facilities and Equipment
required_sections.guidance: List available facilities, equipment, computing resources. Explain why institution can successfully conduct this work.

required_sections.name: Budget
required_sections.guidance: Complete NASA budget forms. Justify all costs. NASA has specific policies on facilities, equipment, subcontracts.

required_sections.name: Data Management Plan
required_sections.guidance: Describe: data types collected, standards used, data sharing, archiving, preservation. Address any mission data requirements.

required_sections.name: Prior NASA Support
required_sections.guidance: If you have had prior NASA awards, describe results and how this proposal builds on prior work.

required_documents.name: SF-424 Application for Federal Assistance
required_documents.description: Standard federal application form required for NASA grant submissions (submitted via NSPIRES or Grants.gov)
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate SF-424 Application for Federal Assistance for a NASA research grant

required_documents.name: NASA Budget Forms
required_documents.description: Budget detail forms including SF-424A and any NASA-specific budget templates showing costs by category
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate budget forms for a NASA research grant application

required_documents.name: Technical Proposal
required_documents.description: Detailed technical description of the proposed research including objectives, approach, innovation, work plan, deliverables, and relevance to NASA mission
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate a technical proposal for a NASA research grant addressing objectives, approach, and NASA mission relevance

required_documents.name: Key Personnel Biographical Sketches
required_documents.description: Biosketches for the PI and all key personnel describing education, experience, publications, and relevant NASA or applied research expertise
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate biographical sketches for key personnel on a NASA grant application

required_documents.name: Current and Pending Support
required_documents.description: List of all current and pending funding for each key investigator including source, amount, period, and relationship to this proposal
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate a current and pending support document for key personnel on a NASA grant

required_documents.name: Facilities and Equipment Description
required_documents.description: Description of laboratory, computing, and fabrication facilities and equipment available for the project
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate a facilities and equipment description for a NASA grant application

required_documents.name: Export Control Certification
required_documents.description: Certification of compliance with ITAR (22 CFR 120-130) and EAR (15 CFR 730-799) export control regulations; must disclose all foreign nationals on the team
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload your signed export control certification. Must disclose all foreign national team members and any ITAR/EAR-controlled technology. May require Technology Assistance Agreement for foreign participation.

davis_bacon.description: NASA rarely funds construction projects.

section_3.description: HUD requirement. Not applicable to NASA.

nepa.description: NASA research grants generally do not trigger NEPA review.

buy_america.description: Not typically applicable to NASA research grants.

single_audit.description: Organizations spending $1M+ in federal funds per year must have a Single Audit.

irb.description: IRB approval required for research involving human subjects.

iacuc.description: IACUC approval for research involving vertebrate animals.

matching.description: Most NASA research grants do not require matching. EPSCoR may require state match.

itar.description: International Traffic in Arms Regulations (22 CFR Parts 120-130) apply to research involving defense articles or technology on US Munitions List. Cannot share ITAR-controlled data with foreign nationals without license. Criminal penalties up to $1M fine and 20 years imprisonment.

ear.description: Export Administration Regulations (15 CFR Parts 730-799) apply to dual-use technologies. Check Commerce Control List. Fundamental research exclusion available.

new_technology_reporting.description: Must report ALL new technology developed under the grant via NASA New Technology Reports, not just patentable inventions.

indirect_cost_rules.notes: NASA follows 2 CFR 200. SBIR and EPSCoR have hard dollar caps where indirect costs reduce direct research spending. EPSCoR: $200K/year or $750K over 3 years total.

submission_portal.name: NSPIRES
submission_portal.notes: NSPIRES is NASA proprietary system, different from Grants.gov. ROSES has 50+ sub-programs per year. SBIR uses sbir.nasa.gov. Transitioning to BAA model in 2026.

nasa.ai_context: NASA proposals must demonstrate direct relevance to NASA mission areas and strategic priorities. ROSES (Research Opportunities in Space and Earth Sciences) has 50+ sub-programs annually. Export control (ITAR/EAR) is MORE restrictive for NASA than most agencies because space technology overlaps with missile technology (USML Category IV). Foreign nationals on the team must be disclosed and may require Technology Assistance Agreements. EPSCoR is limited to 28 eligible jurisdictions. NSPIRES is a separate system from Grants.gov with its own learning curve. New Technology reporting is mandatory for all inventions.

formatting_rules.notes: Default formatting. 12pt Times New Roman, 1-inch margins, single-spaced.

dod.name: DOD (Department of Defense)

prerequisites.name: SAM.gov Registration
prerequisites.description: Active registration with CAGE code (Commercial and Government Entity) required.

prerequisites.name: Unique Entity Identifier (UEI)
prerequisites.description: 12-character identifier from SAM.gov, replaced DUNS in April 2022.

prerequisites.name: Grants.gov Account
prerequisites.description: For research grants submitted through Grants.gov.

prerequisites.name: DSIP Registration
prerequisites.description: Defense SBIR/STTR Innovation Portal (dodsbirsttr.mil) required for DOD SBIR/STTR proposals.

prerequisites.name: Facility Security Clearance
prerequisites.description: Required only if work involves classified information. Processing takes 3-12+ months.

prerequisites.name: CMMC Certification
prerequisites.description: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification for contracts handling CUI. Phased implementation.

required_sections.name: Project Summary
required_sections.guidance: Unclassified summary: project title, objectives, technology areas, expected deliverables. No proprietary information.

required_sections.name: Technical Proposal
required_sections.guidance: Must address: (1) objectives/technical approach, (2) innovation/unique contributions, (3) deliverables, (4) milestones, (5) risk management, (6) dual-use potential if applicable.

required_sections.name: Statement of Work
required_sections.guidance: Detailed task breakdown: tasks, subtasks, deliverables, timeline. Use standard SOW format with task numbers.

required_sections.name: Personnel Qualifications
required_sections.guidance: Describe key personnel: education, experience, relevant technical expertise. Show team capability to perform work.

required_sections.name: Budget
required_sections.guidance: Detailed budget with justifications. DOD has specific cost principles - may require certified cost/pricing data.

required_sections.name: Facilities
required_sections.guidance: Describe: laboratory, computing, fabrication capabilities. Show ability to handle classified work if applicable.

required_sections.name: Security Requirements
required_sections.guidance: If project involves classified work, describe: facility clearance level, ability to handle classified information.

required_documents.name: SF-424 Application for Federal Assistance
required_documents.description: Standard federal application form required for DOD research grant submissions
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate SF-424 Application for Federal Assistance for a DOD research grant

required_documents.name: Budget Forms
required_documents.description: Budget detail forms showing costs by category; DOD may require certified cost or pricing data for larger awards
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate budget forms for a DOD research grant application

required_documents.name: Technical Proposal
required_documents.description: Detailed technical description including objectives, approach, innovation, deliverables, milestones, and risk management
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate a technical proposal for a DOD research grant addressing objectives, approach, and risk

required_documents.name: Key Personnel Qualifications
required_documents.description: Resumes and biosketches for the PI and all key personnel showing relevant technical expertise and experience
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate key personnel qualifications for a DOD research grant application

required_documents.name: ITAR/Export Control Compliance Certification
required_documents.description: Certification of compliance with ITAR (22 CFR 120-130) and EAR regulations; must disclose all foreign nationals and confirm ability to handle export-controlled information
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload signed ITAR/export control compliance certification. Must list all foreign national team members. Many DOD SBIR topics are explicitly ITAR-restricted.

required_documents.name: Cybersecurity Plan (CMMC Compliance)
required_documents.description: Plan demonstrating compliance with NIST SP 800-171 for safeguarding Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) per DFARS 252.204-7012 and CMMC requirements
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload your cybersecurity plan demonstrating NIST SP 800-171 compliance. Include your CMMC certification level or timeline for achieving required level. System Security Plan (SSP) and Plan of Action and Milestones (POA&M) may be required.

required_documents.name: Subcontractor Information
required_documents.description: Details on all proposed subcontractors including scope of work, budget, qualifications, and security clearance status
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate subcontractor information for a DOD research grant application

required_documents.name: Cost Accounting Standards Certification
required_documents.description: CAS certification or disclosure statement as required for DOD grants; modified CAS applies to awards over $7.5M, full CAS over $100M
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload your CAS certification or CAS Disclosure Statement. Must have DCAA-adequate accounting system before award.

davis_bacon.description: DOD grants are primarily for basic research, not construction.

section_3.description: HUD requirement. Not applicable to DOD.

nepa.description: DOD research grants generally do not trigger NEPA.

buy_america.description: Not typically applicable to DOD research grants (applies more to contracts).

single_audit.description: Organizations spending $1M+ in federal funds per year must have a Single Audit.

irb.description: IRB approval required for research involving human subjects.

iacuc.description: IACUC approval for research involving vertebrate animals.

matching.description: DOD research grants generally do not require matching.

itar.description: ITAR (22 CFR Parts 120-130) applies broadly. Many SBIR topics explicitly marked ITAR-restricted. Foreign nationals cannot work on ITAR-restricted topics. Must disclose ALL foreign nationals in proposal.

dfars.description: Defense Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplement adds defense-specific rules including cybersecurity (NIST SP 800-171), export control, and technical data rights. DFARS 252.204-7012 requires safeguarding CUI.

cas.description: Cost Accounting Standards apply to awards over $7.5M (modified CAS) or $100M (full CAS). 19 standards covering cost tracking and allocation. CAS Disclosure Statement required.

dcaa_audit.description: Defense Contract Audit Agency can and will audit books. Must have DCAA-adequate accounting system before award. Annual incurred cost submissions required.

indirect_cost_rules.notes: DOD follows FAR Part 31 and 2 CFR 200 for grants. DCAA negotiates and audits rates. Provisional rates used during performance, final rates established after audit. SBIR Phase I: $275K cap (DOD). Phase II: $1.75M cap. Indirect costs within caps.

submission_portal.name: Grants.gov / DSIP
submission_portal.notes: Research grants through Grants.gov. SBIR/STTR through Defense SBIR/STTR Innovation Portal (DSIP). DARPA has program-specific portals. Each service branch runs its own programs.

dod.ai_context: DOD grants are primarily for BASIC RESEARCH. Applied research uses contracts or Other Transactions (OTs). DOD is the LARGEST SBIR/STTR funder (~60% of all federal SBIR). ITAR restrictions are pervasive -- many topics restrict foreign national participation. DFARS cybersecurity requirements (NIST SP 800-171) apply to CUI. Cost Accounting Standards apply to large awards. DCAA audits are thorough -- need adequate accounting system BEFORE award. DARPA grants are limited to nonprofits and universities (companies get contracts). Each service branch (Army, Navy, Air Force) runs SBIR independently.

formatting_rules.notes: Default formatting. 12pt Times New Roman, 1-inch margins, single-spaced.

fema.name: FEMA (Federal Emergency Management Agency)

prerequisites.name: SAM.gov Registration
prerequisites.description: Active registration at SAM.gov is required. Allow 4+ weeks for new registration.

prerequisites.name: Unique Entity Identifier (UEI)
prerequisites.description: 12-character identifier from SAM.gov.

prerequisites.name: FEMA GO Registration
prerequisites.description: FEMA Grants Outcomes portal required for all FEMA grants. AOR must create account and assign roles.

prerequisites.name: FEMA-Approved Hazard Mitigation Plan
prerequisites.description: State, tribal, or local plan must be FEMA-approved and current (not expired). Valid for 5 years. Required for HMGP, BRIC, FMA.

required_sections.name: Project Abstract
required_sections.guidance: Brief summary: project description, location, hazards addressed, target population, expected outcomes.

required_sections.name: Project Narrative
required_sections.guidance: Include: (1) statement of problem/need, (2) scope of work, (3) methodology, (4) timeline/milestones, (5) evaluation criteria, (6) sustainability.

required_sections.name: Budget
required_sections.guidance: Detailed budget. FEMA grants require cost sharing in many programs - document match clearly. Justify all costs.

required_sections.name: Budget Justification
required_sections.guidance: Explain each line item. Show costs are reasonable, necessary, and allowable under FEMA guidelines.

required_sections.name: Environmental/Historic Preservation
required_sections.guidance: Complete EHP screening. Describe: project location, environmental impacts, compliance with NEPA, Section 106 (historic), floodplain/wetlands.

required_sections.name: Maintenance and Sustainability
required_sections.guidance: Describe how project will be maintained after FEMA funding ends. Document long-term viability, operations costs.

required_sections.name: Risk Analysis
required_sections.guidance: For some programs: describe hazard risk being addressed, probability, consequences, how project reduces risk.

required_documents.name: SF-424 Application for Federal Assistance
required_documents.description: Standard federal application form required for FEMA grant submissions
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate SF-424 Application for Federal Assistance for a FEMA grant

required_documents.name: SF-424A Budget Information - Non-Construction Programs
required_documents.description: Standard federal budget form detailing costs by category and funding period
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate SF-424A Budget Information form for a FEMA grant

required_documents.name: SF-424B Assurances - Non-Construction Programs
required_documents.description: Standard assurances for non-construction programs requiring authorized representative signature
required_documents.upload_instructions: Download SF-424B from Grants.gov, have your Authorized Representative sign it, and upload the signed form.

required_documents.name: Benefit-Cost Analysis (BCA)
required_documents.description: Analysis using FEMA BCA Toolkit (Version 6.0+) demonstrating that project benefits exceed costs with BCR >= 1.0 using 3.1% discount rate; required for all HMA project subapplications
required_documents.upload_instructions: Complete your Benefit-Cost Analysis using the FEMA BCA Toolkit (Version 6.0+). BCR must be >= 1.0 using 3.1% discount rate. Upload the BCA output report.

required_documents.name: Scope of Work
required_documents.description: Detailed scope of work describing project activities, methodology, timeline, milestones, and deliverables
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate a scope of work for a FEMA hazard mitigation or preparedness grant

required_documents.name: Detailed Cost Estimate
required_documents.description: Itemized cost estimate supporting the budget with unit costs, quantities, and sources for cost data
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate a detailed cost estimate for a FEMA grant project

required_documents.name: Hazard Mitigation Plan Certification
required_documents.description: Certification that the applicant's jurisdiction has a FEMA-approved and current hazard mitigation plan (valid for 5 years); prerequisite for HMGP, BRIC, and FMA
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload proof of your FEMA-approved hazard mitigation plan including plan name, approval date, and expiration date. Enhanced plans unlock 20% HMGP funding vs. 15%.

required_documents.name: Environmental and Historic Preservation (EHP) Screening Form
required_documents.description: FEMA EHP screening form providing information about the project location, environmental impacts, and historic preservation considerations for FEMA environmental review per 44 CFR Part 10
required_documents.upload_instructions: Complete and upload the FEMA EHP screening form. Include project location maps, photos, and information about environmental and historic resources in the project area. FEMA conducts the environmental review.

davis_bacon.description: Applies to FEMA-funded construction projects over $2,000. Weekly certified payroll reports required. Applies to HMGP, BRIC, and Public Assistance construction.

section_3.description: HUD requirement. Not applicable to FEMA.

nepa.description: FEMA conducts environmental review (not the applicant) per 44 CFR Part 10. Categorical Exclusions common for standard mitigation. Section 106 historic preservation review required.

buy_america.description: Not a standard FEMA requirement for mitigation grants.

single_audit.description: Organizations spending $1M+ in federal funds per year must have a Single Audit.

irb.description: Not applicable to FEMA grants.

matching.description: HMGP: 75% federal / 25% non-federal (up to 90% for small impoverished communities). Preparedness SHSP/UASI: no match. EMPG: 50/50.

benefit_cost_analysis.description: Benefit-Cost Ratio must be >= 1.0 using FEMA BCA Toolkit (Version 6.0+). Discount rate 3.1%. Required for all HMA project subapplications.

hazard_mitigation_plan.description: FEMA-approved hazard mitigation plan is a prerequisite. Plans valid 5 years. Enhanced plans unlock 20% HMGP funding (vs 15%).

indirect_cost_rules.notes: FEMA follows 2 CFR 200. Management costs capped at 15% for state and 10% for local (different from indirect costs). Standard negotiated rate or de minimis applies.

submission_portal.name: FEMA GO
submission_portal.notes: FEMA GO is separate from Grants.gov. Some programs use Grants.gov for initial application. Local governments must apply through state emergency management agency.

fema.ai_context: FEMA grants are heavily intermediated -- local governments CANNOT apply directly and must go through their State Emergency Management Agency. HMGP is only available after a Presidential disaster declaration. Benefit-Cost Analysis is a unique FEMA requirement using their specific BCA Toolkit. Hazard mitigation plan is a prerequisite -- no plan, no funding. BRIC program was canceled in April 2025. NEPA review is conducted by FEMA (not the applicant). Cost share is typically 75/25 federal/non-federal. Private nonprofits have very limited direct access.

formatting_rules.notes: Default formatting. 12pt Times New Roman, 1-inch margins, single-spaced.

dol.name: Department of Labor (DOL)

prerequisites.name: SAM.gov Registration
prerequisites.description: Active registration at SAM.gov is required.

prerequisites.name: Unique Entity Identifier (UEI)
prerequisites.description: 12-character identifier from SAM.gov.

prerequisites.name: Grants.gov Account
prerequisites.description: AOR registration required for competitive grant applications.

prerequisites.name: ETA-9130 Reporting Capability
prerequisites.description: Must be able to submit quarterly financial reports via ETA system.

required_sections.name: Project Summary
required_sections.guidance: Overview: project goals, target population, services provided, expected outcomes, geographic service area.

required_sections.name: Statement of Need
required_sections.guidance: Describe: labor market need, target population characteristics, gaps in services, why federal intervention needed. Use data.

required_sections.name: Project Description
required_sections.guidance: Include: (1) program design, (2) services/activities, (3) target population, (4) partnerships, (5) timeline, (6) expected outcomes.

required_sections.name: Budget
required_sections.guidance: Detailed budget. Many DOL programs require cost sharing - document match. Follow DOL cost principles.

required_sections.name: Budget Justification
required_sections.guidance: Justify all costs. Explain how costs relate to program design and outcomes. Address any match requirements.

required_sections.name: Organizational Capacity
required_sections.guidance: Describe: organizational experience, staff qualifications, governance, financial management, previous grant performance.

required_sections.name: Evaluation Plan
required_sections.guidance: Describe: performance measures, data collection, reporting requirements. DOL has specific performance accountability requirements.

required_sections.name: Leveraging Resources
required_sections.guidance: Document other resources: matching funds, in-kind, partnerships that supplement federal funds.

required_documents.name: SF-424 Application for Federal Assistance
required_documents.description: Standard federal application form required for all DOL competitive grant submissions
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate SF-424 Application for Federal Assistance for a DOL workforce grant

required_documents.name: SF-424A Budget Information - Non-Construction Programs
required_documents.description: Standard federal budget form detailing costs by category and funding period
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate SF-424A Budget Information form for a DOL grant

required_documents.name: SF-424B Assurances - Non-Construction Programs
required_documents.description: Standard assurances for non-construction programs requiring authorized representative signature
required_documents.upload_instructions: Download SF-424B from Grants.gov, have your Authorized Representative sign it, and upload the signed form.

required_documents.name: SF-LLL Disclosure of Lobbying Activities
required_documents.description: Required if award exceeds $100,000; discloses any paid lobbying activities
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate SF-LLL Disclosure of Lobbying Activities form for a DOL grant

required_documents.name: Project Narrative
required_documents.description: Detailed narrative describing program design, services, target population, partnerships, timeline, and expected workforce outcomes
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate a project narrative for a DOL workforce development grant

required_documents.name: Budget Narrative
required_documents.description: Detailed justification for each budget line item explaining how costs relate to program design and workforce outcomes
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate a budget narrative for a DOL workforce grant justifying all cost categories

required_documents.name: Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) Certification
required_documents.description: Certification of compliance with EEO requirements per 29 CFR Part 38 including designation of Equal Opportunity Officer and maintenance of nondiscrimination policies
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload signed EEO certification including name and contact information for your designated Equal Opportunity Officer. Must demonstrate compliance with 29 CFR Part 38.

required_documents.name: WIOA Performance Accountability Plan
required_documents.description: Plan describing how the project will track and report on the six WIOA Primary Indicators of Performance: employment 2nd/4th quarter after exit, median earnings, credential attainment, measurable skill gains, and employer effectiveness
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate a WIOA Performance Accountability Plan addressing the six primary indicators of performance for a DOL grant

davis_bacon.description: Applies to YouthBuild construction training and any DOL grant with construction component. Participants doing construction must be paid prevailing wages.

section_3.description: HUD requirement. Not applicable to DOL.

nepa.description: DOL grants generally do not trigger NEPA review.

buy_america.description: Not applicable to DOL workforce grants.

single_audit.description: Organizations spending $1M+ in federal funds per year must have a Single Audit.

irb.description: Not applicable to DOL workforce grants.

matching.description: Some DOL programs require cost sharing. EMPG requires 50/50. Check specific FOA.

wioa_performance.description: Six WIOA Primary Indicators: employment 2nd/4th quarter after exit, median earnings, credential attainment, measurable skill gains, employer effectiveness. Failure to meet targets triggers sanctions.

eeo.description: Must designate Equal Opportunity Officer, maintain EEO policies, provide language access for LEP individuals, and conduct periodic self-assessments per 29 CFR Part 38.

indirect_cost_rules.notes: DOL follows 2 CFR 200 with DOL-specific deviations in 2 CFR Part 2900. Some FOAs cap admin/indirect costs at a percentage of the award (often 10%). Check each FOA.

submission_portal.name: Grants.gov / DOL EGrants
submission_portal.notes: Competitive grants submitted through Grants.gov. Post-award management through DOL EGrants system.

dol.ai_context: DOL proposals must demonstrate strong employer engagement and demand-driven workforce development. WIOA performance indicators are critical -- must track and report six specific outcome measures. YouthBuild requires construction skills training with Davis-Bacon compliance. Many programs require serving specific populations: disconnected youth, returning citizens, veterans. Logic models or theories of change typically required. DOL strongly emphasizes partnerships with employers and workforce boards. Quarterly financial (ETA-9130) and performance reports required.

formatting_rules.notes: Default formatting. 12pt Times New Roman, 1-inch margins, single-spaced.

doj.name: DOJ (Department of Justice)

prerequisites.name: SAM.gov Registration
prerequisites.description: Active registration at SAM.gov is required.

prerequisites.name: Unique Entity Identifier (UEI)
prerequisites.description: 12-character identifier from SAM.gov.

prerequisites.name: Grants.gov Account
prerequisites.description: For initial application submission.

prerequisites.name: JustGrants Account
prerequisites.description: DOJ JustGrants system (justicegrants.usdoj.gov) for application completion and award management.

prerequisites.name: DOJ Certified Standard Assurances
prerequisites.description: Must acknowledge DOJ standard assurances in JustGrants.

required_sections.name: Project Abstract
required_sections.guidance: Brief summary: project title, goals, target area/population, expected outcomes. No proprietary information.

required_sections.name: Statement of the Problem
required_sections.guidance: Describe the crime/public safety problem: data, trends, root causes, impact on community. Show why intervention needed.

required_sections.name: Project Design
required_sections.guidance: Describe: (1) goals/objectives, (2) activities/services, (3) how addresses problem, (4) innovation, (5) partnerships, (6) timeline.

required_sections.name: Budget
required_sections.guidance: Complete detailed budget. DOJ requires cost sharing for some programs. Justify all costs.

required_sections.name: Budget Justification
required_sections.guidance: Explain each line item. Show costs are reasonable, necessary, allowable. Address match if required.

required_sections.name: Organizational Capacity
required_sections.guidance: Describe: experience with similar projects, key personnel, financial management, ability to implement.

required_sections.name: Evaluation Plan
required_sections.guidance: Describe: performance measures, data collection, analysis, how you will measure success.

required_sections.name: Sustainability
required_sections.guidance: Describe how project will continue after federal funding ends. What resources will sustain operations?

required_documents.name: SF-424 Application for Federal Assistance
required_documents.description: Core application form required for all federal grants. Submit via Grants.gov at least 48 hours before JustGrants deadline.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate SF-424 Application for Federal Assistance from organization profile, project info, and contact data

required_documents.name: Disclosure of Lobbying Activities
required_documents.description: Discloses any paid lobbying activities. Required if award exceeds $100,000. Submit 'N/A' if no lobbying.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate SF-LLL Disclosure of Lobbying Activities form from organization lobbying data

required_documents.name: Budget Detail Worksheet
required_documents.description: Detailed line-item budget covering Personnel, Fringe, Travel, Equipment, Supplies, Construction, Consultants/Contracts, Other Costs, and Indirect Costs.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate DOJ Budget Detail Worksheet from budget data with all cost categories and justifications

required_documents.name: DOJ Certifications Regarding Lobbying, Debarment, Suspension, Drug-Free Workplace
required_documents.description: Signed certification document covering lobbying, debarment/suspension, drug-free workplace, and coordination requirements.
required_documents.upload_instructions: Sign and upload the DOJ Certifications document from JustGrants. Must be signed by the Authorized Representative.

required_documents.name: Applicant Disclosure of Duplication in Cost Items
required_documents.description: Discloses any pending applications at other agencies for the same or similar project to avoid duplicate funding.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate Duplication Disclosure listing any pending applications for similar projects at other federal agencies

required_documents.name: Applicant Disclosure of Pending Applications
required_documents.description: Lists all other federal/state funding applications currently pending for this or related projects.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate Pending Applications Disclosure listing all other funding applications currently pending

required_documents.name: Research and Evaluation Independence and Integrity Statement
required_documents.description: Statement addressing actual/potential conflicts of interest for research or evaluation projects. Includes mitigation plan.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft a Research Independence and Integrity Statement addressing potential conflicts of interest and mitigation measures

required_documents.name: Organizational Chart
required_documents.description: Chart showing reporting structure and placement of project staff within the organization.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate an organizational chart showing reporting structure and project staff placement

required_documents.name: Position Descriptions for Key Personnel
required_documents.description: Job descriptions for each key position funded by the grant, including duties, qualifications, and time commitment.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate position descriptions for key personnel including duties, qualifications, and percent time on project

required_documents.name: Curriculum Vitae / Resumes for Key Personnel
required_documents.description: Resumes or CVs for all key personnel implementing the project. Include description of experience/skills for unfilled positions.
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload resumes/CVs for all key personnel. For unfilled positions, upload a description of required experience and skills.

required_documents.name: Indirect Cost Rate Agreement (NICRA)
required_documents.description: Federally-approved indirect cost rate agreement, or certification of de minimis 15% rate per 2 CFR 200.414(f).
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload your current federally-approved NICRA, or certify use of 15% de minimis rate. De minimis only available if org has NEVER had a negotiated rate.

required_documents.name: Financial Management and System of Internal Controls Questionnaire
required_documents.description: Questionnaire assessing financial management capability including high-risk disclosure from other agencies.
required_documents.upload_instructions: Complete the Financial Management Questionnaire in JustGrants. Assesses your organization's financial controls and management capability.

required_documents.name: Timeline / Project Schedule
required_documents.description: Project timeline showing key activities, milestones, and completion dates by month or quarter.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate a project timeline with key activities, milestones, and target completion dates organized by quarter

required_documents.name: Letters of Support
required_documents.description: Letters from law enforcement, community organizations, or government officials confirming partnership and support.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft a letter of support from [partner] confirming their partnership and support for [project] as part of a DOJ grant application. Include the partner's role (law enforcement, community organization, or government office), their specific commitment, and how the partnership advances public safety or justice goals. Label clearly as DRAFT — TO BE CUSTOMIZED AND SIGNED.
required_documents.upload_instructions: After generating a draft, print it, have the partner review and edit it, then get it signed on their letterhead. Upload the signed version here.

required_documents.name: MOU with Partner Agencies
required_documents.description: Memoranda of understanding with law enforcement, courts, or service providers detailing roles, services, and duration.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) between [applicant organization] and [partner agency] for a DOJ grant project. Include the purpose of the partnership, roles and responsibilities of each party, specific services to be provided, duration of the agreement, data sharing provisions, and any resource commitments. Label clearly as DRAFT — TO BE CUSTOMIZED AND SIGNED BY BOTH PARTIES.
required_documents.upload_instructions: After generating a draft, print it, have both parties review and edit it, then get it signed by authorized representatives from both organizations on official letterhead. Upload the signed version here.

davis_bacon.description: DOJ grants rarely involve construction.

section_3.description: HUD requirement. Not applicable to DOJ.

nepa.description: DOJ grants typically do not trigger NEPA.

buy_america.description: Not applicable to DOJ grants.

single_audit.description: Organizations spending $1M+ in federal funds per year must have a Single Audit.

irb.description: IRB approval required for research and evaluation grants (NIJ, OJJDP research) involving human subjects. Privacy certificate also required.

matching.description: Some programs require cost sharing. Check specific NOFO. JAG formula does not require match.

vawa_confidentiality.description: OVW grants require strict victim confidentiality per VAWA. Cannot disclose personally identifying information without informed consent. Cannot require victims to participate in services as condition of receiving other services.

performance_reporting.description: All DOJ recipients must collect and report performance data demonstrating measurable results.

indirect_cost_rules.notes: DOJ accepts negotiated NICRA or de minimis 15%. DOJ Financial Guide 2024 governs cost principles. Check specific NOFO for additional restrictions.

submission_portal.name: JustGrants
submission_portal.notes: Initial application via Grants.gov, then complete and manage in JustGrants. JustGrants replaced GMS as of October 2020.

doj.ai_context: DOJ proposals must address crime/public safety problems with data on trends and impact. Key offices: BJA (justice assistance), OVW (violence against women), OJJDP (juvenile justice), COPS (policing), OVC (victims of crime). OVW grants have strict VAWA confidentiality requirements -- victim information cannot be shared without informed consent. JustGrants is the primary portal (different from Grants.gov). DOJ Financial Guide governs cost principles. Performance measurement plans required. Faith-based organizations eligible but cannot use funds for religious activities.

formatting_rules.notes: Double-spaced. Page limits strictly enforced. Times New Roman preferred.
